name: Milk Chocolate Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the almonds in a shallow pan in a preheated 350°F (175°C) oven for 12 to 15 minutes, shaking the pan once or twice. Remove from the oven when you can smell toasted almonds through the door.
2. Let the almonds cool completely.
3. Place the chocolate pieces into the bowl of a food processor with the metal chopping blade. Process until fine and powdery.
4. Sift together the flour, baking soda, salt, cocoa, and sugar. Add about one cup of these sifted ingredients to the chocolate mixture.
5. Mix in the remaining sifted dry ingredients and cooled almonds.
6. Beat the eggs with vanilla extract in a small bowl.
7. Stir the egg mixture into the dough until combined.
8. Shape two loaves: Place plastic wrap on a work surface, then form strips of dough (13-14 inches long) on each piece of wrap. Flatten tops and lift sides to press smooth shapes.
9. Form each shape into an even strip about 1/2 inch thick, with squared ends.
10. Transfer the shaped dough to cookie sheets lined with parchment or foil. Place in the freezer for at least 2 hours until firm.
11. Preheat the oven to 300°F (150°C). Line two large cookie sheets with parchment or foil.
12. Turn the strips onto the prepared sheets, diagonally placing each one. Remove from the oven and flip using a spatula.
13. Cool the cooled strips on racks for 10 minutes, then cut into 1/2 to 3/4 inch slices using a bread knife.
14. Place the sliced biscotti on unlined cookie sheets with space between them.
15. Bake for another 40 minutes, flipping the sheets during baking. Let cool and store in an airtight container.
